headline: Plans underway for blighted South Burlington businesses
quote: “We’re excited to see buildings that were vacant for a while have new tenants.” *per WCAX*
commentary: Looks like the neighborhood might finally get a glow-up after years of graffiti and tumbleweeds. Fingers crossed for a new pizza spot that doesn’t vanish before we even get a slice.

headline: Vermont’s top two teams in Lego competition will be traveling to national events
quote: “The STEM and leadership skills the students develop through this program are exactly what we need for our future workforce.” *per the Burlington Free Press*
commentary: Give these kids extra applause—and maybe some celebratory maple creemees. They’re set to show off their engineering talents in Houston and Florida, so root for them while quietly envying their winter escapes.

headline: Vermont Law School offers help with taxes
quote: “It’s one of many sites set up and open across the state.”*per WCAX*
commentary: If the thought of your W-2 gives you heart palpitations, Vermont Law’s got your back. Expect them to handle the complicated stuff while you daydream about springtime on Church Street.

headline: Co-founder of Burlington-based company focused on breastfeeding to take over as CEO
quote: “One of the co-founders of Mamava, Christine Dodson, is taking over in February as chief executive officer…” *per the Burlington Free Press*
commentary: Mamava pods have popped up in airports and offices like mushrooms after a rainstorm. Let’s cheer on a homegrown business that makes life easier for new moms—score one for #BTV innovation.

headline: Instead of ‘stealing Greenland from Denmark’ Bernie Sanders thinks Trump should steal this
quote: “In Denmark, everyone is guaranteed health care, college education is free, parents receive 1-year of paid paternity leave & workers don’t make less than $22 an hour.”*per USA TODAY*
commentary: Bernie’s got jokes—and possibly the best off-the-wall pitch to repurpose Uncle Sam’s time. Let’s just hope we can keep it civil, or at least keep any strong winds from blowing away the memo.

headline: There’s a new music festival in Burlington, and it’s called Groundhog (Guess when it is?)
quote: “Then Schuster thought of the Waking Windows festival that for more than a decade has unfolded over multiple days in multiple venues.” *per the Burlington Free Press*
commentary: February in Vermont can feel like six more weeks of “not going outside,” so might as well boogie indoors. Looks like folks in the 802 found a new reason to break out the flannel and dance away those winter blues.

headline: UVM holds off Maine after late three by Bogues
quote: “Bogues later iced the game at the free throw line.”*per WCAX*
commentary: Move over, maple swirl—Bogues might be our new local hero. The Cats are clawing their way up the standings, so let’s hope they keep draining those buzzer-beaters.

headline: How UVM sports teams fared Jan. 31-Feb. 1: Schedule, scores, results
quote: “Bogues’ 3-pointer via a Blake assist broke a 49-all deadlock with 33 seconds left in regulation as Vermont halted a two-game losing skid.” *per the Burlington Free Press*
commentary: The scoreboard might have been tighter than downtown parking, but a win’s a win. Now if only we could break our mid-winter losing streak against lingering ice.

headline: New Housing Report Outlines Future of Abandoned Vermont Properties
quote: “Rundown and vacant properties could be on the path to new life. The study notes that nearly 11,000 abandoned homes make up 3 percent of Vermont’s housing stock with a demand for 36,000 new homes by 2029.”*per WCAX*
commentary: It looks like Vermont’s forgotten doorsteps might just get a second chance if the funding holds up.

headline: European Market Moves and Expands Offerings in South Burlington
quote: “‘It feels good to have a nice cozy place to go to for a cup of coffee or a bite of something that reminds us of home,’ a longtime customer shared as the market unveiled its new expanded space.”*per Seven Days*
commentary: This is not just an upgrade in square footage; it is a hearty serving of community spirit with fresh flavors and nostalgic recipes making a comeback.

headline: Sanders Hawley Push Bill to Cap Credit Card Interest Rates
quote: “‘When large financial institutions charge over 25 percent interest on credit cards, they are not engaged in the business of making credit available,’ warned Sanders while Hawley lamented, ‘Working Americans are drowning in record credit card debt while the biggest issuers get richer by hiking their rates to the moon.’”*per Burlington Free Press*
commentary: Bipartisan frustration is in the air if only the lobbyists would take a hint and ease up on the sky high rates.

headline: Chittenden County Home Sales Soar to New Heights
quote: “Median home sales reached $520,000 in November up 17.5 percent from October as data reveals single family homes and condos are fetching significantly higher prices.”*per Burlington Free Press*
commentary: Whether you are on the buying or selling side these rising numbers underscore a sizzling local market even if they make window shopping a bit more challenging.

headline: New England Gas Prices Tumble But Are Still Steady
quote: “Regional gas prices dipped to an average of $3.00 per gallon for regular fuel just a hair below last week’s $3.02 according to the U.S. Energy Information Administration.”*per Burlington Free Press*
commentary: The pump is showing a slight reprieve but do not get too comfortable; our gas stations still keep us on our toes.

headline: Back Nine Indoor Golf Lounge Opens with Nine Simulators
quote: “‘You have your own space to create your own environment’ said Chris Burke as he unveiled Back Nine Indoor Golf Lounge featuring nine simulators and a full service bar designed for both pros and rookies alike.”*per MyNBC5*
commentary: For those missing a day on the links this new indoor venue is a hole in one opportunity to mix leisure with a dash of local flair.

headline: Vermont Tourism Generates Record $4 Billion in 2023
quote: “Vermont’s tourism sector rebounded in 2023 generating a record $4 billion in revenue and drawing nearly 16 million visitors a full return to pre pandemic levels.”*per Burlington Free Press*
commentary: Our scenic state continues to charm visitors from near and far proving that a dash of Vermont magic is always in style.

headline: UVM Researchers Investigate Pain Treatment Options
quote: “Fentanyl is commonly used to treat pain in trauma patients but can lower oxygen levels and blood pressure,” Wolfson said in a statement. “There’s also a risk of long-term opioid dependency.” *Per Burlington Free Press*
commentary: This study hopes to figure out if ketamine might be an equally potent alternative—minus some of fentanyl’s more challenging side effects. If it can help treat pain without fueling the opioid crisis, that’s a conversation worth having.

headline: UVM Medical Center Reduces Dependence on Traveling Nurses
quote: “We couldn’t have done it without them, but we also need to look at the long term for what’s best for Vermonters,” said UVMMC’s Sarah Hoffman. *Per WCAX*
commentary: With travel nurses costing a small fortune, the hospital is recruiting permanent staff to save money and ensure continuity of care. Let’s hope this trend holds since nurse retention is always good medicine.

headline: Proposal to Convert Empty State Offices into Housing
quote: “So we can save taxpayer dollars on maintaining state buildings that aren’t full, stimulate business activity downtown, and fill a hole in the housing that’s so desperately needed these days,” said Rep. Conor Casey.*Per WCAX*
commentary: Hey, if we can transform empty cubicles into cozy apartments, that might just help with Vermont’s perennial housing crunch; and turn ghostly hallways into vibrant new neighborhoods.

headline: Nectar’s to Honor Legendary Sound Engineer Sergei Ushakov
quote: “To say that Sergei was a pillar of Burlington’s music community would be an understatement,” Nectar’s posted on social media. *Per Burlington Free Press*
commentary: On February 15, the iconic music venue will host an all-day tribute to the man who helped countless local acts find their groove. Expect a live “superjam” that would make even the pickiest audiophile smile.

headline: Rare Pancake Ice Appears at Leddy Beach
quote: “They’re constantly bumping into each other, and so any rough edges get rounded out, and you form these pancakes,” said oceanographer Ted Maksym. *Per Seven Days*
commentary: File under “Things you never thought you’d see in Vermont.” If you missed this fleetingly delicious-looking phenomenon, keep an eye on the lake, Mother Nature might dish up seconds if conditions align again.

headline: Local Man’s Cross-Country Trek for Democracy
quote: “I didn’t meet a single person who thought [Congress was] doing their job,” said Rick Hubbard, 83, who finished a 3,081-mile journey across 14 states. *Per Other Paper SBVT*
commentary: Determined to nudge our government back to “we the people,” Hubbard strolled from LA to D.C. to spread the word. Clearly, some folks are willing to go the distance, literally, when it comes to civic engagement.

headline: Lake Placid Hosts Historic Women’s World Cup Ski Jumping
quote: “I’m confident when competition begins, the prize awards for men and women will be equal,” said event spokesperson Darcy Norfolk. P*er WCAX*
commentary: Just a hop and a skip across the lake, Lake Placid is making headlines with a first-ever U.S. women’s ski jumping World Cup. If your winter weekend needs an adrenaline fix, you know where to find it.

headline: UVM in Talks for Men’s Soccer Coach Contract
quote: “Dow just finished up the second year of a four-year deal… and acknowledged the entire coaching staff has gotten external interest.” *Per NBC5*
commentary: After a dream season, it’s no shock that UVM’s beloved coach is drawing attention. Fans are crossing their fingers (and tail feathers, for that Catamount flair) for a deal that keeps him on the pitch right here.

headline: Women’s Hockey Falls to BC Despite Early Goal
quote: “Vermont was able to capitalize… 5:24 into action on the power play, scoring first in Beecher’s 100th career game.” *Per UVM Athletics*
commentary: A tough final score for the Catamounts, but an early tally in a milestone matchup is always something to celebrate. Here’s hoping they bounce back at Gutterson with a home crowd advantage next time.

headline: Protesters Rally Against $100 Million Prison Proposal in South Burlington
quote: “Protesters held signs that read, ‘Build Homes, Not Prisons,’ voicing their concerns over the potential expansion of the state’s prison system.”*(per NBC5)*
commentary: Activists are pushing to redirect funds into housing, education, and other social supports. Organizers vowed to keep the conversation going and say there are more rallies ahead.

headline: Cappadocia Bistro to Bring Turkish Street Food to Burlington
quote: “The Oktay family, who own three Turkish eateries and two imported goods shops in Vermont, will add a Burlington restaurant specializing in Turkish street food and flatbreads this spring.”*(per Seven Days)*
commentary: Expect a piping-hot array of Turkish flatbreads, chicken doner wraps, and other street-food favorites. If you need a reason to stroll Church Street when the weather’s brisk, this might be it.

headline: Half of Burlington Is Green Space: City Planners Seek Public Input
quote: “Almost 50% of Burlington’s geographic area is an open or green space, something the Parks, Recreation and Waterfront Department is looking for public input on preserving.”*(per Burlington Free Press)*
commentary: Help shape the updated Open Space Plan. Should we plant more trees in the Old North End? Add new public trails or small parks? Let the city know by filling out their survey.

headline: Vermont Special Olympics Hosts 30th Annual Penguin Plunge
quote: “The Vermont Special Olympics held its 30th Annual Penguin Plunge on Saturday. Hundreds of participants and spectators showed up to the icy waters in support of a meaningful cause.”*(per NBC5)*
commentary: Brave souls took an early dip into Lake Champlain and blew past their $600,000 fundraising goal. Proceeds help 1,000+ athletes statewide, proving once again that Vermonters’ hearts are warmer than our February lakes.

headline: Higher Ground Mulls Move to Burlington’s Pine Street
quote: “Higher Ground is considering a move to Pine Street in Burlington. The venue would complement Pine Street’s vibrant arts scene.”*(per Seven Days)*
commentary: One of the area’s favorite music venues might set up shop near Burlington City Arts. The move hinges on a zoning tweak, but fans are hoping it all works out.

headline: Breeze Airways Adding Direct Flight to Charleston, SC, Out of Leahy BTV
quote: “Breeze Airways will begin seasonal nonstop service from Burlington to Charleston, South Carolina, on May 30. The route marks the first time since 1990 that Vermonters can fly directly to 19 destinations.”*(per Burlington Free Press)*
commentary: If you’re itching for a quick southern getaway, you’ll soon have a direct route to sunny Charleston. Breeze says they’ve seen strong demand out of Burlington, so pack those flip-flops (or keep your parka handy for the return flight).

headline: Road Salt Contamination in Focus for Legislators
quote: “Salt can be found just about everywhere outside in the winter — cars, roads, sidewalks — but where does it all go come spring? The answer often is streams, rivers and lakes...”*(per WCAX)*
commentary: Too much chloride in our waterways threatens aquatic life and can corrode infrastructure. Look for new salt-use training programs for road crews — and consider shoveling more, salting less at home.

headline: Catamounts Win Thriller in Double Overtime Against Jacksonville (Women’s Lacrosse)
quote: “Sophomore Lydia Doraz scored her first career hat-trick and freshman Caitlin Haggerty scored a hat-trick and a game-winning goal in double overtime.”*(per UVM Athletics)*
commentary: That’s how you start the season off with a bang. Two hat-tricks in a single game never hurts, either.

headline: TJ Hurley’s 21 Points Leads Vermont Past UAlbany (Men’s Basketball)
quote: “TJ Hurley had a team-high 21 points which marks his 7th game this season scoring 20+ points.”*(per UVM Athletics)*
commentary: The Cats keep climbing the conference standings. Keep an eye on Hurley — if he’s hot, Vermont’s in good shape.

headline: Women’s Hockey Secures 3-2 Victory Over #15 Boston College
quote: “Vermont battled to a 3-2 victory over #15 Boston College…with two goals in the opening minute of both the first period and the second period.”*(per UVM Athletics)*
commentary: A statement win against a ranked foe has fans fired up for the season’s final push.

headline: Second Period Blitz Leads Vermont to 5-3 Win at #9 UMass Lowell (Men’s Hockey)
quote: “Vermont scored three goals in 95 seconds at the start of the second period on route to a 5-3 win.”*(per UVM Athletics)*
commentary: Big second periods have been Vermont’s secret weapon lately. They’ll look to build on that with playoffs looming.

headline: UVMMC Urges Pediatric RSV Immunization for Newborns and Infants
quote: “RSV is the most common reason for children under one year old to be admitted to the hospital,” per Vermont Business Magazine
commentary: A new monoclonal antibody has significantly cut RSV admissions, and doctors say now’s the time to make sure your baby’s protected. Here’s hoping we see fewer of those anxious trips to the hospital for good!

headline: Burke to Serve as Burlington Interim Police Chief
quote: “Mayor Emma Mulvaney-Stanak announced Wednesday that Shawn Burke will fill the role,” per WCAX.
commentary: Burke steps in after leading the South Burlington force and 20 years with Burlington’s department prior to that. Fingers crossed he brings good energy to BPD and continues to build trust around town.

headline: Burlington Councilors Consider Increasing Funding to the Housing Trust Fund
quote: “We have an incredibly successful model that was created in the Housing Trust Fund,” per WAMC.
commentary: Councilors voted to look into boosting the city’s commitment to affordable housing. Given the tough housing market (and eye-watering rents), a little extra support could go a long way.

headline: UVM Launches Hospitality Management Certificate to Support Tourism Workforce
quote: “Hospitality is one of Vermont’s largest economic sectors, and there is a high demand for developing a skilled workforce,” per Vermont Business Magazine.
commentary: This new certificate promises real-world training for folks eyeing a career in hotels, resorts, and restaurants—great news for anyone looking to jump into Vermont’s bustling tourism scene (and hopefully snag some sweet industry perks).

headline: UVM Health Network Physician Honored with National Award for Work in Addiction
quote: “The NAADAC ... awarded Mashkuri the 2024 Davida Coady Gorham Medical Professional of the Year Award,” per Burlington Free Press.
commentary: Dr. Mashkuri’s community-driven initiatives have paved new paths for substance use treatment and recovery in central Vermont. It’s always uplifting to see local medical pros recognized for making a difference.

headline: Vt. Outdoor Recreation Leaders Push Strategic Plan
quote: “Let’s protect our outdoor spaces, boost community wellness, and spark new opportunities,” per WCAX.
commentary: The Move Forward Together plan aims to keep the state’s $4 billion recreation economy rolling along while protecting the spaces we all love to hike, bike, and ski. Now if only it came with a magical solution for those muddy spring trail closures.

headline: Catamount Men’s Basketball Hosts New Hampshire Thursday Evening
quote: “Vermont (13-11) (6-3 AE) will host New Hampshire (6-19) (4-6 AE) on Thursday evening,” per UVM Athletics.
commentary: UVM looks to keep momentum after a solid win against UAlbany. Swing by Patrick Gym if you need a midwinter hoops fix, or tune in on ESPN+ to cheer from your couch.

headline: Mikaela Shiffrin Teams Up for Gold at World Championships
quote: “Mikaela Shiffrin and teammate Breezy Johnson Tuesday won the first team combined race in World Alpine Skiing Championships history,” per WCAX.
commentary: From Burke Mountain Academy to world champion again—Shiffrin seems unstoppable (even after her recent crash). She’s not just collecting medals; she’s cementing her status as a Vermont legend.

headline: Country artist Brantley Gilbert to play 2025 Champlain Valley Fair in Vermont
quote: “The Champlain Valley Exposition said country artist Brantley Gilbert will perform on Saturday, Aug. 23.” (per MyNBC5)
commentary: Gilbert’s rap-rock edge has made him a country star, and if you want in on that August show, keep an eye out for tickets going on sale next Friday. Dust off those cowboy boots and get ready for a rowdy late-summer night.

headline: UVM joins ranks of top research universities with ‘R1’ designation
quote: “What this does is put us in the very top tier of research institutions in the country,” said UVM Larner College of Medicine Dean Richard Page. (per WCAX)
commentary: It’s a big leap for the university, promising broader grant funding and bolstering its reputation for innovation. Good news for curious minds and for the local economy that thrives on cutting-edge research.

headline: Burlington to open emergency cold weather shelter amid subzero temperatures
quote: “The emergency shelter will be at the Miller Center at 130 Gosse Court and will be open from Monday evening at 5 p.m. to Tuesday at 8 a.m.” (per MyNBC5)
commentary: With overnight wind chills expected to dip below zero, the city’s stepping up to keep folks safe. If you know someone in need of a warm place this weekend, pass along the info—and remember, being neighborly can make a world of difference when the thermometer drops.

headline: Burlington’s incoming interim police chief says he will not seek to stay on in the role
quote: “Burke has agreed to step in as interim chief on March 24 and serve…not expected to stay on.” (per VTDigger)
commentary: The department continues to fine-tune its recruitment strategy while navigating a transitional period. Let’s hope fresh leadership helps steady the ship and keep our city streets safe.

headline: How you can participate in the Great Backyard Bird Count
quote: “Whether it’s their window feeder or their backyard, they can observe and record birds via eBird or Merlin.” (per WCAX)
commentary: A great chance to help scientists track bird populations. All you need is a phone app and a few minutes of bird-watching. You can even participate from your armchair—citizen science never felt so cozy.

headline: What To Do: Saturday, February 15
quote: “Events run Saturday from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. and Sunday from 9 a.m. to 8 p.m.” (per WCAX)
commentary: Ice skating, fireworks, dog sled rides—the perfect trifecta for a mid-winter weekend. If you’d rather stay in, there are plenty of other indoor adventures, too. Check local listings if you’re traveling.

headline: Wood podiums for fourth time this season during day one of Middlebury Carnival
quote: “Mackenzie Wood secured his fourth podium appearance…placing third in the slalom race.” (per UVM Athletics)
commentary: UVM Skiing stays hot on the slopes—and on the scoreboard. With multiple top-10 finishes, the Catamounts look ready to give fans an exciting rest of the season.

headline: Dominant Offense Leads Catamounts in a Win Against Le Moyne
quote: “Lauren Lefebvre, Lydia Doraz, and Maddie Erskine all recorded hat-tricks for the Catamounts.” (per UVM Athletics)
commentary: Women’s Lacrosse is off to a high-scoring start. With three different players netting hat-tricks, it’s safe to say the team found its groove early.

headline: Balanced Offense Boosts Catamounts in Win Over New Hampshire
quote: “Shamir Bogues had a team-high six assists.” (per UVM Athletics)
commentary: UVM Men’s Basketball is sharing the ball and racking up the points—nothing like good chemistry to keep the momentum going in conference play.

headline: Priede’s Career Night Leads Catamounts Past Wildcats
quote: “Priede was the Catamount's top scorer with 15 on seven field goals.” (per UVM Athletics)
commentary: Women’s Hoops continues to impress—anytime you see a career-high performance from a key player, it bodes well for the rest of the schedule.

headline: At Vermont Statehouse, UVM Health Network CEO Faces Tough Questions — and Praise
quote: “The health network... is the state’s largest, and a major player in its health care landscape.”*(per Valley News)*
commentary: Some lawmakers were openly skeptical about recent service cuts, while others offered more supportive feedback. The ongoing conversation signals that statehouse pressure on the network will remain high as officials debate funding, bonuses, and Vermont-to-New-York cash flow.

headline: Charlotte Ferry Crossing Closed Monday
quote: “The Lake Champlain Transportation Company issued the alert around 7 a.m.”*(per MyNBC5)*
commentary: Ice in McNeil’s Cove forced the shutdown, leaving travelers to seek alternate routes across the lake. It’s a good reminder that winter crossing can be unpredictable, so check for updates before heading out.

headline: National Sandwich Chain Opens First Vt. Store
quote: “Monday is the grand opening of Firehouse Subs first-ever restaurant in Vermont, located at Maple Tree Place in Williston.”*(per WCAX)*
commentary: Food lovers can add a new stop to their rotation. If you’re in the area, try a sub and know that a portion of each purchase supports public safety groups.

headline: New Commission Plans to Grow Shelburne Tourism Revenue
quote: “They’ll be working with a new non-profit called Experience Shelburne.”*(per WCAX)*
commentary: Town officials say the focus is on attracting more visitors and supporting local businesses. If you see a flurry of new events popping up in Shelburne, this commission might be the reason.

headline: S. Burlington Makes Submitting Permits, Registrations Easier for Residents
quote: “Before, all applications had to be submitted on paper and in-person at city hall.”*(per WCAX)*
commentary: Now everything can be done online. That’s good news for anyone who prefers to skip the drive and handle things with a few clicks.

headline: Burlington Nonprofit Searching for Mentor Volunteers
quote: “Mercy Connections is looking for volunteers for their Vermont Women’s Mentoring Program.”*(per WCAX)*
commentary: They support women reentering society after incarceration, offering guidance and resources for a stable transition. If this speaks to you, reach out — there’s training available for those ready to help.

headline: Vermont School Officials Say Transgender Athletes Will Compete
quote: “The Vermont Agency of Education... wrote in an email to school leaders that protections for LGBTQIA+ students remain enshrined in Vermont law.”*(per Seven Days)*
commentary: Local administrators are reaffirming their commitment to an inclusive sports environment. For now, the state stands by its policy that welcomes transgender girls on girls’ teams.

headline: Tire Shop Owner Removes Billboards After State Warning
quote: “They’d hung for weeks on half a dozen hay wagons in Chittenden and Addison counties, drawing the ire of some Vermonters.”*(per Seven Days)*
commentary: Vermont’s strict billboard ban is alive and well. After a notice from officials, the shop owner decided to comply and take them down, proving it’s often easier to keep your ads online than alongside a country road.

headline: Men’s Basketball: TJ Hurley’s 24 Points Leads Vermont Over Maine
quote: “TJ Hurley led the Catamounts in scoring with 24 points... Sam Alamutu had a career-high 13 rebounds and a career-high 7 assists.”*(per UVM Athletics)*
commentary: The Catamounts walked away with a strong road win, holding second place in the conference. Keep an eye on Hurley’s hot streak if you’re planning to catch the next home game.

headline: Women’s Basketball: Catamounts Use Strong Defense and Efficient Shooting to Defeat Maine
quote: “Anna Olson led the way with 19 points as Vermont held Maine to only eight first-half points.”*(per UVM Athletics)*
commentary: Shutting down an opponent like that takes discipline, and the team proved they have it. With their momentum growing, expect a fun match on Thursday.

headline: Men’s Hockey: Late Power Play Goal Lifts Merrimack Past Vermont
quote: “Caden Cranston scored the game-winning goal on the power play with 34 seconds left in regulation.”*(per UVM Athletics)*
commentary: A tough finish for the Catamounts, but they put up a fight on the road. Next weekend’s home series will offer a chance for redemption against a top conference rival.

headline: Women’s Hockey: Women’s Hockey Scores Late, Holy Cross Wins 3-1 on Sunday
quote: “Vermont fell 3-1 to Holy Cross... but found the back of the net with just over a minute left.”*(per UVM Athletics)*
commentary: They nearly staged a comeback, though the empty-netter sealed it for the Crusaders. There’s still time to regroup before their final regular-season home stand later this week.

headline: Grammy Winner Gary Clark Jr. to Play Shelburne Museum
quote: “A multiple Grammy-winning artist is coming to Vermont this summer, and tickets to the show will be available soon. Singer and rock guitarist Gary Clark Jr. will play a show at Shelburne Museum as part of the Ben & Jerry's Concerts on the Green series, according to an announcement from Higher Ground.”*(per MyNBC5)*
commentary: Looking for a must-see outdoor show? Circle June 27 on your calendar and prep your lawn chair—Shelburne Museum is set to host a sweet summertime jam.

headline: Bread for Peace: Faith Communities Unite for a Good Cause
quote: “On February 23, members of a Burlington synagogue and a Quaker meeting house will join forces to bake bread for a good cause. Proceeds from sales of challah, pita bread and gluten-free oat bread will benefit a pair of humanitarian aid organizations serving Israel, Gaza and the West Bank.”*(per Seven Days)*
commentary: It’s heartwarming to see neighbors from different backgrounds rolling up their sleeves together. Volunteer bakers are bridging divides and proving bread can bring us all to the table.

headline: Weekend Snow Totals Gave Us a Blast From Winters Past
quote: “The National Weather Service at the Burlington International Airport released statistics Monday afternoon showing totals from around the state for the storm that began Feb. 15 and lasted into Feb. 17. Widespread 12-18 inches were seen across the region with lower amounts across southern Vermont due to freezing rain and sleet.”*(per Burlington Free Press)*
commentary: If your shovel got a workout, you’re not alone. Whether you reveled in the powder or felt it in your lower back, Vermont’s old-school winter vibe is definitely back in action.

headline: Nusantara Brings the World to Essex
quote: “Nusantara, which means ‘archipelago’ in ancient Javanese, opened at the Essex Experience shopping center in November 2022. The retailer sells antique furniture, art, clothing, jewelry and knickknacks from 70 countries, with an emphasis on Asia.”*(per Seven Days)*
commentary: If cabin fever has you dreaming of faraway places, a trip to Nusantara might scratch that itch. Wandering through its eclectic wares can feel like traveling the globe without leaving Chittenden County.

headline: Make-A-Wish Vermont Debuts Immersive ‘Wish Discovery Theater’
quote: “Make-A-Wish Vermont unveiled a new 180-degree immersive theater this weekend that’s designed to inspire children with critical illnesses to imagine a wish that could help in their recovery. The project benefited from a $125,000 in-kind donation from Vermont Construction Co., according to a news release from Make-A-Wish Vermont.”*(per Burlington Free Press)*
commentary: Even a small spark of hope can brighten a child’s toughest days. Kudos to everyone involved for making dreams a bit more vivid—right in Shelburne’s own backyard.

headline: Jay Peak Snowfall Could Break Records
quote: “Wright says the mountain received 550 inches about two decades ago and that if the pace keeps up, they could be on the way to breaking that record this year. ‘Those guys get quite a bit of snow, so to be in front of them is a big deal,’ said Jay Peak president Steve Wright.”*(per WCAX)*
commentary: Why travel west for deep powder if Jay might go all-in this season? Keep that gear handy—this winter might have a few more surprises for us before the thaw.

headline: UVM ROTC Earns Northeast’s Top Honors
quote: “UVM officials say the ROTC won this year’s General Douglas MacArthur Award for leadership for the third time in the last 10 years. The program was chosen based on achievements including members’ GPAs, retention rates, and training and fitness.”*(per WCAX)*
commentary: Apparently, training in Vermont’s frigid conditions builds discipline—and accolades. Big congrats to the cadets and staff who keep that tradition of excellence alive in Burlington.

headline: South End Innovation Project Moves Forward
quote: “The Burlington City Council Tuesday night unanimously signed off on an agreement that will allow the next phase of planning for the so-called South End Innovation District. That includes conducting various studies surrounding stormwater capacity, roads and different housing types.”*(per WCAX)*
commentary: It’s still a big parking lot today, but maybe not forever. Keep an eye on Lakeside Avenue—2026 might bring fresh housing, jobs, and a new vibe to the South End.

headline: Catamounts Open America East Championships With Record Relay
quote: “For the second straight year the Catamounts opened the America East Swimming and Diving Championships setting a varsity record in the 200 Medley Relay with a time of 1:41.53. After the first day of competition the Catamounts sit in fourth place with 84 points.”*(per UVM Athletics)*
commentary: If this is how UVM swims at the start of the meet, the rest of the conference better brace for a splash. A strong relay plus more finals ahead spells excitement for Catamount fans.

headline: Rice Coach Paul Pecor Notches 400th Victory
quote: “Tuesday night, Rice rolled past Colchester for a 77-48 decision at Bill Hammond Gym, securing Pecor’s 400th career victory at the Catholic school. In his 22nd season with the Green Knights, Pecor owns a 400-91 record — an 81.2% winning percentage — with 10 Division I state titles, four runner-up finishes and 18 Final Four appearances.”*(per Burlington Free Press)*
commentary: That’s a lot of wins—and a lot of green banners. Rice basketball fans won’t be short on reasons to cheer as the season hits the playoff stretch.

headline: Jazz Night with Ray Vega (Jazz)
quote: **Time:** 8:30 PM
commentary: **Location:** Hotel Vermont, Burlington | **Cost:** Free

headline: Live Music: Jazz with Alex Stewart and Friends (Jazz)
quote: **Time:** 8:30 PM
commentary: **Location:** The 126, Burlington | **Cost:** Free

headline: $1 Billion Sale of Vermont Information Processing Will Lead to Worker Windfalls
quote: **“Hundreds of employee-owners of the Colchester-based firm are expected to receive life-changing payments.”** (per *Seven Days*)
commentary: Some employees could see up to $10 million each after the private equity firm Warburg Pincus buys the company for about $1 billion, including debt. The sale’s windfalls highlight Vermont’s growing focus on employee stock ownership plans.

headline: Burlington Telecom Scheduled Maintenance Next Week
quote: **“Work will take place from roughly 2:00 a.m. to 4:00 a.m. on February 25, 26, and 27.”** (per *MyNBC5*)
commentary: BT customers may experience brief overnight network disruptions as system upgrades are performed. Officials say the maintenance is part of ongoing efforts to provide better broadband services.

headline: Vampire Weekend to Play Show in Essex Junction, Vermont
quote: **“The alt-rock group will be stopping in Vermont this spring as part of their latest tour.”** (per *NBC5*)
commentary: Frontman Ezra Koenig and crew are set to perform on the Midway Lawn at the Champlain Valley Expo on May 27, with tickets on sale starting Feb. 28. Expect a memorable outdoor show as part of their “Only God Was Above Us” tour.

headline: Vermont’s Greenest Building Awards Competition Now Open for Submissions
quote: **“This statewide contest recognizes buildings that excel in water, health, transportation, affordability, and proven energy performance.”** (per *Vermont Business Magazine*)
commentary: Applicants have until March 21 to apply, with winners announced April 17 in Burlington. It’s a key event for architects, developers, and property owners dedicated to sustainable design in Vermont.

headline: Several New Shops, Restaurants Coming to Finney Crossing in Williston
quote: **“Three new businesses are slated to open once a new mixed-use building is completed on the property later this year.”** (per *NBC5*)
commentary: Expect a boutique women’s clothing store, an independent bookstore and café, plus a casual Italian-American eatery among the new arrivals. Each locally owned shop hopes to enhance Williston’s bustling retail scene.

headline: UVM Health Network to Maintain Dialysis Centers
quote: **“Officials say no other providers stepped forward to take over, so the clinics in Newport, Rutland, and St. Albans will remain open.”** (per *WCAX*)
commentary: Amid cost-cutting measures, the network initially planned to shutter the centers but will continue operations at a loss. Patient advocates praised the move, calling it critical to rural healthcare access.

headline: Judge Rejects Effort to Block Noncitizen Voting in Burlington
quote: **“A Vermont judge dismissed the case, noting local school votes are considered municipal, not statewide.”** (per *Valley News*)
commentary: The challenge came from a national conservative group arguing the city’s charter amendment violated the state constitution. But Burlington joins Montpelier and Winooski in allowing noncitizen residents to vote in local elections.

headline: A Cannabis-Themed Magic Act Will Play Tricks on Your Mind
quote: **“Las Vegas-based magician Ben Zabin’s ‘Smokus Pocus’ performs illusions with bongs, lighters, and plenty of 420 humor.”** (per *Seven Days*)
commentary: Zabin brings the show to Main Street Landing on Friday, Feb. 21. You don’t have to partake to enjoy — the illusions combine classic sleight-of-hand with a modern twist sure to dazzle.

headline: Men’s Hockey: Catamounts Return to Gutterson to Host #2 Boston College
quote: **“The teams face off at Gutterson Fieldhouse this weekend after UVM’s strong run on the road.”** (per *UVM Athletics*)
commentary: Following an impressive stretch of away wins, the Catamounts look to upset a top-ranked Boston College squad. The two-game series marks UVM’s first home appearance in over a month.

headline: Women’s Basketball: Catamounts Fall to Bryant, 61-53
quote: **“Nikola Priede recorded her second career double-double, but UVM’s offense couldn’t keep pace.”** (per *UVM Athletics*)
commentary: Despite Priede’s 17 points and 12 rebounds, Vermont dropped to 10-3 in America East play. The Cats head to UMass Lowell next, hoping to regain momentum before the conference tournament.

headline: Swimming & Diving: Catamounts Set New Conference Relay Record
quote: **“Vermont’s 200 Freestyle Relay team swam a 1:32.17, a new America East benchmark.”** (per *UVM Athletics*)
commentary: Powered by standout performances across multiple events, UVM holds first place in the conference championships. The meet continues through the weekend, with the Cats eyeing more podium finishes.

headline: Men’s Basketball: Defense Propels Vermont Over Bryant, 59-55
quote: **“TJ Hurley’s 17 points led UVM, and a late steal sealed the victory at Patrick Gym.”** (per *UVM Athletics*)
commentary: Tight defense in the final minutes helped the Catamounts hold Bryant’s offense in check. Up next, UVM hosts UMass Lowell Saturday, looking to continue its run toward the top of the conference standings.

headline: State Representative Revives UVM Enrollment Cap Bill
quote: “A bill seeking to place an enrollment cap on UVM until Burlington’s housing vacancy rate rises above 5% was introduced in the Vermont House of Representatives on Feb. 7.”*(per The Vermont Cynic)*
commentary: Some folks argue it will help preserve affordable housing options, while others worry it may restrict the university’s growth. Legislators are balancing community interests and UVM’s desire to expand.

headline: Powerball Ticket Sold in Vermont Worth $1M
quote: “The ticket was sold at Glover’s Family Market and Deli in Newport Center. It matched five of the numbers in Saturday night’s drawing but missed matching the Powerball number.”*(per WCAX)*
commentary: This means someone up north is suddenly feeling luckier. Double-check your pockets, because missing out on a million bucks would be quite a bummer.

headline: Mitch McConnell to Retire. Will VT’s Bernie Sanders Be Next?
quote: “U.S. Sen. Mitch McConnell… announced Thursday that he would not seek reelection… Bernie Sanders does not appear to be considering retirement.”*(per Burlington Free Press)*
commentary: Regardless of Sanders’ age, he keeps pressing on with that familiar vigor. For those banking on Bernie stepping aside soon, don’t bet on it just yet.

headline: Vermonters Could Be Affected by Slashed USAID Jobs
quote: “Most of the workforce at USAID is now on administrative leave, and experts say that could impact Vermonters.”*(per WCAX)*
commentary: Companies around here that secure government contracts may face cutbacks, leaving some locals concerned about paychecks and benefits. Global aid might be distant, but the ripple can reach us right at home.

headline: Our Katharine Huntley Makes Ice Skating Look Easy
quote: “She said it was her first time skating out on the frozen lake. And she makes it look so easy!”*(per WCAX)*
commentary: Nothing like a graceful glide on Lake Champlain to remind us why winter can be magical. Maybe we’ll all be inspired to lace up and find a patch of ice before the thaw sets in.

headline: Vice President Vance to Visit Vermont on Ski Trip
quote: “U.S. Vice President JD Vance and his family plan to ski at Sugarbush this weekend... Drivers should expect some delays during an expected motorcade.”*(per WCAX)*
commentary: If you see flashing lights on the way to the slopes, that might just be DC royalty in town. Keep an eye out and maybe watch out for any potential traffic from it!

headline: Mikaela Shiffrin Gets Historic 100th World Cup Race Win
quote: “Shiffrin’s record-extending 100th career World Cup race win... She also tied an all-time World Cup record for men and women, as her 155th career top-3 finish matched Swedish great Ingemar Stenmark.”*(per WCAX)*
commentary: We might not be international skiing champions here at home, but Shiffrin’s feats give us all something to cheer about. She’s a reminder that on the slopes, Americans still know how to set records.

headline: Phone Lines Temporarily Down at So. Burlington Police Dept.
quote: “The outages are planned for 7 a.m. to 9:30 a.m. and 5 p.m. to 7:30 p.m. on Monday.”*(per WCAX)*
commentary: If you tried calling and got static, that’s why. The good news is they’ve set up alternate numbers, and 911 calls are unaffected. Hopefully the upgrades will be worth the brief hassle.

headline: New Bill Aims to Reduce Road Salt Runoff in Vermont
quote: “Road salt runoff is making waterways like Lake Champlain saltier, putting wildlife and even drinking water infrastructure at risk.”*(per WCAX)*
commentary: Balancing safe, ice-free roads with protecting the lake is a typical Vermont juggling act. If you see salt trackers around town, it’s all part of the plan to keep our beloved waters healthy.

headline: Friendship Blossoms After Student Helps UVM Employee Recover After Fire
quote: “After a UVM Dining employee lost her home to a fire, a student stepped up to help, and a friendship blossomed.”*(per WCAX)*
commentary: It’s uplifting to see neighbors supporting neighbors. If you needed a reason to smile or do something kind this week, let this remind you that small gestures can become big moments.

headline: Why Some Vermont Police Are Rethinking Pursuit Policies
quote: “When a suspect flees from police, officers are left with a decision to make: chase or stand down.”*(per WCAX)*
commentary: Some departments want stricter guidelines, citing public safety and tragic outcomes in recent pursuits. Expect discussions of balancing firm law enforcement with making sure no one gets hurt in a chase.

headline: Catamounts Fall to Marist on the Road
quote: “Vermont (2-1) took a loss in Poughkeepsie, NY on Saturday afternoon, falling to Marist (1-3)…”*(per UVM Athletics)*
commentary: They’ll look to turn things around next game. It’s early in the season, and there’s plenty of time to regroup and push forward.

headline: Catamounts Defeat UMass Lowell in Final Regular Season Road Game
quote: “The Catamounts have clinched a top-two seed in the playoffs.”*(per UVM Athletics)*
commentary: Momentum is on their side as the postseason nears. Keep an eye on them if you love a defensive showdown.

headline: Women’s Hockey Delivers Strong Effort in Senior Day Loss to #14 Northeastern
quote: “Vermont opened scoring in the second period thanks to Natalie Zarcone’s second goal of the campaign...”*(per UVM Athletics)*
commentary: Despite the scoreboard, the Catamounts showed flashes of top-notch play. They’ll have a chance for redemption in the Hockey East playoffs.

headline: Catamounts Finish Second During 2025 EISA Championships
quote: “Justine Clément finished in third place on Saturday afternoon... anchoring the women’s alpine team to the most points out of every school for the second day in a row.”*(per UVM Athletics)*
commentary: Skiing tradition runs deep here, so a second-place finish bodes well for the NCAA Championships ahead.

headline: Track and Field Puts Together Strong First Day at America East Championships
quote: “We had inspired performances from both our teams today.”*(per UVM Athletics)*
commentary: With top-10 marks rolling in, the team is poised for an impressive finish. They’ll look to carry that energy into the next round.

headline: Dominant Offense Leads Vermont in Win Over UMass Lowell
quote: “TJ Hurley led the Catamounts in scoring with 20 points. Sam Alamutu had his 3rd career double-double.”*(per UVM Athletics)*
commentary: The team found their spark, and it showed on the scoreboard. Expect more fireworks if they can keep up that energy.

headline: Cilwik and Gilmore Headline Successful Second Day at America East Championships
quote: “Vermont recorded four all-conference finishers and two individual titles… setting three new school records in the 200, 800, and 4x400 relay.”*(per UVM Athletics)*
commentary: When records start falling, you know the team is peaking. Keep an eye on these Catamounts for some exciting championship meets ahead.

headline: Demolition of blighted South Burlington businesses underway
quote: “This is one of the gateways to our city. To see an investment taking place... is great. It’s a vision we shared with the property owner for a long time.”*(per WCAX)*
commentary: A rundown Pizza Hut and gas station on Shelburne Road are finally coming down. After years of graffiti and boarded-up windows, South Burlington officials say the plan is for commercial space plus 30 new housing units—some of which will be affordable. Here’s hoping that corner of Shelburne Road gets a second life soon.

headline: Warm spell allows Burlington pothole crews to get to work
quote: “We are seeing the freeze-thaw that we expect this time of year and when we are not plowing... we are out filling potholes.”*(per WCAX)*
commentary: The city’s public works teams pounced on this week’s mild temperatures to tackle some of those legendary potholes dotting our streets. They’re patch jobs for now, since asphalt plants are still closed for the season. More permanent fixes roll out once the real spring arrives (whenever that might be).

headline: UVM student wearing heels makes a splash on social media
quote: “It was raining so I didn’t want to walk barefoot... she put on shoes, and I wore her heels.”*(per WCAX)*
commentary: This Valentine’s Day gesture from a Waterbury native studying abroad warmed hearts on both sides of the world. He swapped shoes with his girlfriend because her feet hurt, and a viral video later, his life lesson is to always keep a spare pair. We’ll call that a stride in the right direction.

headline: Burlington airport parking reaches capacity
quote: “With school break, this is one of our busiest travel weeks.”*(per WCAX)*
commentary: It’s a full house at the airport garage and the usual backup lots, thanks to a surge in flights and bigger planes. If you’ve got a trip planned, consider hitching a ride instead of rolling the dice on a parking spot. There’s never been a better time to phone a friend (or that one relative with a flexible schedule).

headline: Improving public transit is the focus of Transit Equity Day in Vermont
quote: “Rosa Parks’ historic protest was against the unequal access of African Americans to public transit. And yet to this day, unequal access... persists.”*(per WAMC)*
commentary: Advocates say a lack of rural bus routes and limited hours hamper Vermonters—particularly those with disabilities or lower incomes. Calls for more funding and better service aren’t new, but the momentum might be growing as ridership numbers creep back up post-pandemic. Time will tell if lawmakers are listening.

headline: PHOTOS: Hundreds gather at Sharp Park for Milton Winter Festival
quote: “Clear skies, mild temperatures and fresh snow made for ideal conditions... families raced down the park’s steep hill.”*(per The Milton Independent)*
commentary: Milton’s annual Winter Festival saw spirited sledding, disc golf competitions, sleigh rides, and a chili cook-off. The turnout was huge, which might be proof that locals embrace winter—at least when there’s a warm drink and fireworks in the picture.

headline: 3 rescued following Mount Equinox plane crash
quote: “Upward of 100 first responders from Vermont and New Hampshire took part in the rescue Wednesday.”*(per WCAX)*
commentary: A small plane went down near Mount Equinox’s summit, but miraculously all three aboard escaped with non-life-threatening injuries. Getting everyone off the mountain in four feet of snow was no small task. Cheers to our first responders for a job well done in tough conditions.

headline: Advocates for school cellphone ban lobby Vt. lawmakers
quote: “It was one of the best things our administration could’ve done for the student body... there’s zero loss but enormous gain.”*(per WCAX)*
commentary: Students and teachers from schools with “bell-to-bell” bans say it boosts focus and reduces drama. Some parents worry about emergencies, but supporters argue the front office is still the fastest way to reach a child if something arises. The debate rages on over how unplugged kids should be from 8 to 3.

headline: Burlington mayor makes pitch for pricey bond measures
quote: “Without this... it will be harder and slower to advance those goals.”*(per WCAX)*
commentary: Burlington residents will decide on nearly $200 million in bonds this Town Meeting Day, mostly to upgrade water and wastewater infrastructure. Rate hikes could follow—one proposal may raise water bills nearly 90% by 2030. City Hall hopes voters see it as an investment in a modern system, not just a blow to the wallet.

headline: Colchester opens new recreation center
quote: “We utilized the schools a lot... so we were missing that facility for people who no longer work. Now we have a place for people to come and recreate during the day.”*(per WCAX)*
commentary: After two decades of planning, the doors are open on Colchester’s new rec center. Residents can now walk an indoor track, shoot hoops, or join training classes—even spin if that’s your speed. Colchester officials say it’s a game-changer for seniors and for folks who hate braving the cold just to get some exercise.

headline: Vermont Begins Mid-Atlantic Trip with a Matchup Against NJIT Thursday Night
quote: “Five different Catamounts earned double-digit scoring against UMass Lowell on Saturday afternoon.”*(per UVM Athletics)*
commentary: The men’s basketball team sits in second place in the America East standings and heads to the Garden State on a hot streak. TJ Hurley’s consistent scoring has been a highlight—fingers crossed for more of the same as they look to secure a postseason advantage.

headline: Catamounts Come Up Short Against Fairfield
quote: “Catamounts started off the match with a goal from Emily Cutler... it was her first collegiate goal.”*(per UVM Athletics)*
commentary: UVM women’s lacrosse fought hard on the road but couldn’t claw back after Fairfield’s late scoring run. Still, there were a few bright spots, including a breakout performance from freshman Caitlin Haggerty. The team heads to Brown next, hoping to turn those flashes of offense into a complete game.

headline: Burlington-area Rap Artist Launches a New Direction
quote: “Sam Paulino, known on stage as Maari, released the self-produced album ‘I Feel Myself Fading.’” (per WCAX)
commentary: Maari is well-known for his work in local hip hop circles, so this ambient pivot has fans curious. If you love discovering artists right when they switch things up, add this album to your weekend playlist.

headline: Burlington Students Hold Media Briefing to Promote Gun Ban
quote: “Student advocates from the University of Vermont and Champlain College showed their support for a proposed ordinance that would prohibit guns in Burlington bars.” (per WCAX)
commentary: Their push comes as the bar-related shooting last year remains on people’s minds. The measure will go before voters on Town Meeting Day, so expect more conversation around this charter change as Election Day approaches.

headline: Burlington Restaurant ‘Closed Indefinitely’ After Fire Marshal Raises Issues
quote: “This business had done unpermitted work inside which led to a capacity concern.” (per Burlington Free Press)
commentary: Longtime patrons hope that these hurdles can be cleared soon. Until then, the downtown spot that once buzzed with late-night slices is dark while owners work toward compliance.

headline: Busy Beavers Play Outsize Role in Vermont Waterway Cleanup
quote: “For the last few years, a family of beavers has made a home in Geprags Community Park in Hinesburg, and they’ve been renovating.” (per VTDigger)
commentary: Turns out, these furry engineers have been creating wetlands that excel at trapping phosphorus. Some folks are even asking whether landowners should be rewarded for hosting helpful beavers. Talk about nature’s own civil engineers.

headline: Patton Oswalt, Leslie Jones, Pulitzer-winning Play Highlight March Arts
quote: “The arts scene will continue to fill out with music, theater, comedy and more.” (per Burlington Free Press)
commentary: March is looking loaded with entertainment, from big-name comedians at the Flynn to local theaters premiering new productions. If you are ready to ditch cabin fever, there is no shortage of places to laugh and cheer.

headline: KidSafe Collaborative Raises $106K for Sally Borden Fund
quote: “We’ve been able to provide meaningful wage adjustments for staff members whose vital work promotes the safety, health and wellbeing of children.” (per Vermont Business Magazine)
commentary: It is a big boost for this Burlington nonprofit, which unites local agencies to protect children. If you care about supporting youth outreach programs, this is a good reminder that community-focused nonprofits are always appreciative of a helping hand.

headline: Gov. Scott Presents Education Transformation Plan to Legislature
quote: “The plan would move the state to a foundation formula by fiscal year 2028, two budget cycles from now.” (per Burlington Free Press)
commentary: Officials hope to streamline school districts and manage rising education costs. Keep an eye on how the legislature reacts, because some of these proposals—like mandatory class sizes—could spark lively debate.

headline: Town Meeting Day to Reveal Balance of Power on Burlington City Council
quote: “Two City Council seats are being vacated, leaving the balance of power between Democrats and Progressives uncertain.” (per Burlington Free Press)
commentary: This year’s votes also include a school budget proposal, three infrastructure bonds, and some notable charter changes. Whether you’re Team Progressive, Team Democrat, or just want that pothole fixed, get your ballot in by Tuesday.

headline: Unlike Musk, Vermont Officials Say Hybrid Work Is Here to Stay
quote: “A recent survey showed 57 percent of state employees still have some kind of hybrid arrangement.” (per WCAX)
commentary: Although remote work empties some local offices—and hits downtown eateries—leaders say it also helps fill state job openings. If you’re missing that midday café bustle, new housing conversions might be on the horizon for vacant office space.

headline: UVM Women's Basketball Beats NJIT
quote: "Four players scored in double figures for the third time this season as Vermont cruised to victory over NJIT, 75-43." (per UVM Athletics)
commentary: The Catamounts continue their impressive run and hope to carry this momentum into Senior Day on Saturday.

headline: UVM Men's Basketball Triumphs Over NJIT
quote: "Shamir Bogues led the Catamounts in scoring with 22 points as Vermont dominated the second half to secure a 71-61 win." (per UVM Athletics)
commentary: A strong second half turned things around for UVM, setting them up nicely for their matchup at UMBC this weekend.

headline: Protesters Line the Streets as JD Vance Vacations in Vermont
quote: “Ski Russia because JD Vance has no friends in Vermont, but he’s got lots of friends in Russia, so he should go there for vacation,” Tekla Van Hoven of Waterbury said. *(per WCAX)*
commentary: Residents from Waitsfield to Warren turned out in the cold to voice their disapproval of the vice president’s policies. If nothing else, Vermont reminded the second-in-command that locals have no fear bringing out signs, flags, and pointed commentary. Way to go Vermont, stand up for what you believe in! Check out photos of protest in the link above.

headline: Protesters Gather in South Burlington as JD Vance Leaves Vermont
quote: “Many held Ukrainian flags in support of the country. Signs read ‘Shame on you Vance’ and ‘Vermonters Will Always Resist Fascism.’” *(per WCAX)*
commentary: A second wave of demonstrations greeted Vance on his way out of the Green Mountains. If the vice president hoped to fly under the radar, these folks made sure the trip ended on a memorable note.

headline: Vt. Maple Sugaring Season Kicks Off With Ceremonial Tree Tapping
quote: “Vermont produces roughly 3 million gallons of maple syrup every season... supplying about half of the country’s crop.” *(per WCAX)*
commentary: Governor Scott signaled the unofficial start of spring’s sweetest tradition. Producers say the below-freezing nights and milder days are the perfect recipe for sap flow. Watch out for steam rising from local sugarhouses as the first batches start boiling.

headline: Sanders to Kick Off Round Two of His ‘Fight Oligarchy’ Tour
quote: “We must fight back,” he said. *(per Seven Days)*
commentary: Senator Bernie Sanders isn’t slowing down at 83. He plans to rally crowds in Michigan and Wisconsin, urging them to stand against billionaire-driven policy decisions. His message? It’s time for working folks to speak up—or risk being overshadowed by big-money influence.

headline: Trump Now Says Tariffs on Canadian Goods Will Start Next Week
quote: “State officials have convened groups of business owners to assess how tariffs would affect the state’s economy. Canada is Vermont’s largest trading partner.” *(per Seven Days)*
commentary: Local businesses that rely on Canadian imports (or attract Canadian tourists) are bracing for potential cost hikes, especially if cross-border shipments get pricier. We’ll see if there’s any last-minute change, but for now, heads up if your supply chain includes Montreal.

headline: With Federal Funding at Risk, UVM Announces 60-Day Hiring Freeze
quote: “Job offers already made will be honored, but the freeze pauses the hiring of all long-term faculty, staff and postdoctoral positions,” states the memo. “We have had no evidence, nothing presented that says that any of UVM funds have been curtailed.” (per VTDigger)
commentary: It’s a stressful turn for anyone hoping to join the university in the near future. Administration says the pause is temporary, but folks on campus are bracing for ripple effects as state and federal budget battles continue.

headline: After Stepping Away from Burlington Politics, Former Mayor Finds New Home in Honors College
quote: “It’s something I’ve worked on one way or another pretty much throughout my whole professional career,” said Miro Weinberger on Vermont’s housing shortage. “It could actually be an opportunity for students to have an experience at influencing the way public policy gets crafted.” (per The Vermont Cynic)
commentary: Weinberger’s pivot to academia keeps him in the conversation about one of the region’s biggest challenges: finding enough places for everyone to live comfortably. Students might end up shaping policy that could affect the rest of us in the coming years.

headline: Democrats Maintain Slim Majority on Burlington City Council; Bonds Pass
quote: “We have been doing ... trying to make progress on the difficult housing issues,” said the local Democratic Party chair. “I’m actually hopeful,” a Progressive Party leader responded. (per WCAX)
commentary: Voters gave a thumbs-up to big spending for city infrastructure and water system upgrades, so expect construction news in your mailbox soon. It’s looking like we’ll stay on track for some overdue repairs, though the monthly bill for water and wastewater is headed upward.

headline: Ben & Jerry’s to Roll Out Electric Scoop Trucks
quote: “The trucks will pop up in Vermont following their debut in Texas this week.” (per WCAX)
commentary: The ice cream legends are leaning into eco-friendly sweet treats on wheels. Be on the lookout for those Rivian-made vans purring quietly around town this spring. Another reason to look forward to warmer days—and Free Cone Day on April 8.

headline: Sanders Offers Independent Response to President’s Joint Address to Congress
quote: “He failed to talk about some of the most important issues facing the working class,” Sanders said, referencing topics like poverty and health care. (per WCAX)
commentary: While official party responses grabbed headlines, our famously independent senator doubled down with a livestream. No surprise to see him emphasizing the plight of everyday folks. Expect those debates to keep heating up as we move deeper into budget season.

headline: Balking at Buying Eggs? Some Vermonters Seek to Go It Alone
quote: “People want to take back some control of their food sources,” said one backyard chicken coop provider. (per WCAX)
commentary: Prices for a carton may still be shocking, so folks are jumping on the backyard-chicken bandwagon. Maybe we’ll see Burlington’s neighborhoods clucking right along with the early spring birds. Just keep your new coop on good terms with the neighbors.

headline: Burlington Voters Say ‘Yes’ on Town Meeting Day
quote: “Burlington voters have made clear their support for this important policy change,” said Mayor Emma Mulvaney-Stanak about the new charter amendment on firearms. (per MyChamplainValley)
commentary: Most of the big ballot items passed easily, continuing the city’s focus on managing housing, infrastructure, and public safety. On redistricting, the voters also handed more authority to the council. We’ll see if the Legislature follows through.

headline: Vito Named Defensive Player of the Year, Catamounts Place Four on All-Conference Teams
quote: “Bella Vito was named Defensive Player of the Year... and Anna Olson was named to the all-conference first team.” (per UVM Athletics)
commentary: The women’s squad keeps racking up honors right before their playoff push. With Vito stealing passes and Olson lighting up the scoreboard, this team is ready to make noise in the conference tournament.

headline: Clément Earns Silver in the Slalom During Day One of the 2025 NCAA Championships
quote: “Graduate Student Justine Clément earned silver in the slalom race... It’s Clément’s fifth podium appearance on the season.” (per UVM Athletics)
commentary: All eyes are on the Catamount skiers as they chase national titles. Clément’s strong run keeps Vermont in the thick of things. Day two of the championships could bring more medals back to Burlington.

headline: Dartmouth, UVM Protests Aim at Federal Research Cuts
quote: “Over 200 people marched from the University of Vermont to downtown to call attention to Trump administration cuts to federal research funding. It comes as the University of Vermont and other schools across the country have announced hiring freezes linked to those cuts.” *per WCAX*
commentary: In typical Burlington fashion, folks are banding together to support science. Organizers say they hope these marches light a fire under local leadership to keep research afloat in Vermont.

headline: From the Publisher: Feeding the Local Economy
quote: “Encounters such as these, or the fear of them, are among the challenges facing Burlington restaurateurs this winter.” *per Seven Days*
commentary: Despite road closures, rising costs, and a good dose of cold weather, restaurants are still hustling. If you can, consider popping in for a meal this weekend — that local lunch might just help keep some of your favorite cafés going.

headline: Vermont Flower Show Comes to Essex Junction This Weekend
quote: “At the show attendees will ‘celebrate and recreate gardening through the ages and discover how we as humans learn from the past as we cultivate our own gardens and plant for the future.’” *per ABC22/FOX44*
commentary: If you’re craving green leaves and fresh blooms, the Champlain Valley Expo is your weekend destination. It’s a reminder that warmer, flower-friendly days truly are on the horizon.

headline: Vt. Law Enforcement Host Hockey Game in Honor of Agent Maland
quote: “All proceeds from a silent auction, merchandise, and a 50/50 raffle go directly to the Maland family.” *per WCAX*
commentary: It’s a community-driven showdown on the ice to commemorate a fallen Border Patrol agent. If you’re near Jay Peak on Sunday, swing by to cheer — and know your support lifts up a family in need.

headline: Vt. Officials Weigh Potential Impact of On-Again, Off-Again Tariffs
quote: “Vermont Commerce Secretary Lindsay Kurrle says while these changes are fluid, they are still working to understand what impact tariffs on Canadian goods and energy would mean for Vermont residents.” *per WCAX*
commentary: If you’ve noticed any unusual upticks in fuel or certain groceries, you might blame the tariff drama. With so much cross-border trade, local businesses and consumers are prepping for whatever the feds decide next.

headline: Scott Pushes Lawmakers to Act This Session on Education Reform
quote: “The plan calls for consolidating the sprawling school bureaucracy down from the current 119 districts to just five, setting minimums on classroom sizes — which will likely trigger the closing of small schools — and throwing out the current funding formula to allocate funding on a per-pupil basis.” *per WCAX*
commentary: The governor wants legislators to move quickly to transform Vermont’s school system. Whether you’re pro or con, keep an eye on how this debate shakes out — it could impact property taxes, district lines, and local communities across the state.

headline: McColgan and Sweet Both National Runner-Ups on Day Two of NCAA Championships
quote: “Annie McColgan and Finn Sweet each placed second in the 7.5K Classic Individual Start from Oak Hill, earning both skiers First Team All-American honors.” *per UVM Athletics*
commentary: A huge showing for UVM Nordic as McColgan and Sweet both snagged silver. The Catamounts are still in the overall hunt, proving once again that Vermont’s love of skiing isn’t just for weekend warriors.

headline: Catamounts Use Dominant Second Half to Defeat #7 UMBC
quote: “Priede led all scorers with 19 and was one of three Vermont players with over 15 points.” *per UVM Athletics*
commentary: A second-half surge sealed the deal for UVM women’s hoops, who are making a serious statement in the postseason. With balanced scoring and strong defense, this squad looks ready for the next round.

headline: Larsson Nathhorst Wins Bronze in the Giant Slalom During Day Three of the 2025 NCAA Championships
quote: “Junior skier Hanna Larsson Nathhorst earned First Team All-American honors as she collected bronze in the giant slalom race.” *per UVM Athletics*
commentary: Nathhorst’s podium finish keeps UVM on the national map for alpine skiing. Watch for more Catamount action as the final NCAA Championship events wrap up this weekend.

headline: Citizen Cider Moving to Flynn Avenue
quote: “Over the next few months we’ll be moving our production facility and pub from its current location on Pine St. to our press house location on Flynn Ave.” *per MSN*
commentary: Longtime patrons can still pop into the Pine Street taproom through late April before it goes dark for a couple of months. The new home near Switchback and Burlington Beer Co. should make quite the craft-beverage hotspot when it reopens. Consider this a sign that Burlington’s South End really is where the fermentation magic happens.

headline: UVM Researchers Unveil AI Tool to Predict Water Quality
quote: “It’s a great watershed because so much has been invested there in monitoring infrastructure… It’s a great proof of concept.” *per Rutland Herald*
commentary: A team at UVM says they can forecast changes in turbidity and other metrics by pulling from decades of data. If it can work for New York City’s complex water system, it may soon help spot trouble areas right in Vermont’s streams and lakes. One more reason to keep an eye on campus research—sometimes the biggest breakthroughs start in our own backyard.

headline: You Could Join Grace Potter on Stage in Vermont
quote: “Their team will sift through the video submissions and choose two people to join Potter on stage. One person will join her in Rutland and another in Burlington.” *per MyChamplainValley*
commentary: If you’ve ever dreamt of belting out a tune—or adding flair with sign language, dance, or lighting design—this might be your moment. Get your submission in by March 15, and you could be tapped to perform alongside a beloved hometown star. Being bold has never been so potentially rewarding, so go ahead and make that audition tape.

headline: The Future of the Burlington Progressive Party
quote: “Part of the negative perception… can be attributed to a 2009 discovery that then-Mayor Bob Kiss diverted city funds… Burlington Telecom.” *per Vermont Cynic*
commentary: A look back at Burlington’s finances shows the party’s reputation took a hit from that old telecom debacle, but current leadership wants to carve a new path. With recent mayors juggling budgets in the tens of millions, voters are watching closely to see who can really deliver fiscal responsibility. Politics aside, it’s a reminder that in Burlington, your track record matters as much as your campaign promises.

headline: Vermont Tech Center Debuts Hybrid EMT Course
quote: “We’re trying to provide as many opportunities for kids to explore what they want to do with their futures.” *per WCAX*
commentary: Green Mountain Tech and Career Center in Hyde Park is hoping this mix of in-person and online sessions will ease our EMS shortage. With some corners of the state waiting long minutes for critical help, new courses like these might be the lifeline needed to staff local squads. If you’ve got the heart for emergency medicine, here’s a flexible way to dive in.

headline: Stowe Foliage Arts Festival Canceled Again
quote: “Organizers said the tent collapse in Stowe had a ‘rippling effect,’ and they would be unable to hold their annual craft fairs in Stowe, Manchester and Lake George.” *per MyNBC5*
commentary: Last fall’s windstorm ended the festival early, and it looks like the fallout is lingering for another year. Craftspeople and vendors are disappointed to lose such a popular showcase, especially during peak leaf-peeping season. Here’s hoping they’ll come roaring back once the supply chain (and the tent stakes) are a bit more secure, literally.

headline: Men’s Basketball: Second Half Surge Propels UVM over New Hampshire
quote: “TJ Hurley led the Catamounts in scoring with 18 points… Ileri Ayo-Faleye recorded his 4th career double-double.” *per UVM Athletics*
commentary: It’s a promising run for the Catamounts, who found a groove just in time for the conference playoffs. Expect the stands at Patrick Gym to stay packed if they keep up this energy. And yes, if you’re reading this before tip-off, they host UMBC tonight (Mar. 10) at 6:00 p.m. in the semifinals—so consider this your sign to cheer them on in person or via ESPN+.

headline: Men’s Ice Hockey: #9 Boston University Skates Past Vermont
quote: “Quinn Hutson had two goals and an assist Saturday night… leading No. 9 Boston University (20-12-2) to a 6-1 win over Vermont.” *per UVM Athletics*
commentary: BU caught the Catamounts on a tough night at Gutterson. With the Hockey East playoffs on the horizon, Vermont’s aiming to regroup and sharpen their defensive play. After all, nobody likes giving up a six-spot at home, especially on Senior Night.

headline: Men’s Lacrosse Falls to UMass
quote: “Despite a four-goal showing from junior midfielder Tristan Whitaker, Vermont fell 11-7 to UMass.” *per UVM Athletics*
commentary: A slow start cost the Catamounts despite Whitaker’s big day. They’ll look to bounce back as they head into conference action against UMBC on March 15. Watch for some tactical adjustments—there’s a lot of talent on this roster just waiting to break out.

headline: Women’s Lacrosse: O’Connor’s Four Goals Lift UVM Past LIU
quote: “Meghan O’Connor led the Catamounts with a career-high four goals.”*per UVM Athletics*
commentary: When an offensive spark like that ignites, it’s a good day to be a Catamount. O’Connor and company are inching towards conference play with serious momentum. Keep tabs on this squad if you want to see more highlight-worthy moves and final scores.

headline: Marlene Tromp Appointed as Next President of UVM
quote: “To be in an environment where people believe that learning and education matters deeply to the future is really exciting to me,” Tromp said Thursday (per WCAX).
commentary: UVM’s newest president arrives with a track record of boosting graduation rates and research at her previous institutions. It’s safe to say the campus is abuzz with both curiosity and cautious optimism. If Tromp can keep those numbers climbing, we might just have another reason to brag about our own backyard Ivy in all but name.

headline: Vermont’s Student Robotics Community Comes of Age
quote: “Champ is a 3-foot-tall metallic structure that resembles a guillotine on wheels, minus the decapitating blade,” (per *Seven Days*).
commentary: These high school engineers sure know how to keep it interesting. In just six weeks, local teams whipped up competition-ready robots and bagged awards across New England. We’ll soon be looking for them on the world stage, showing that Vermont is more than maple syrup and mountain hikes.

headline: Burlington’s Overdose-Prevention Center Unlikely to Open in 2025
quote: “I’m holding as a mayor here both the urgency of bringing this lifesaving tool online but making sure we’re taking the responsible amount of time to do things right,” Mayor Emma Mulvaney-Stanak said (per *Seven Days*).
commentary: The city council and local nonprofits want to get it done, but regulatory hoops and site selection are proving tricky. Neighbors are anxious for a solution to opioid-related emergencies, yet opinions vary on the best location. For many, the hope is that slow and steady will still save lives when this service finally arrives.

headline: Some Vermonters Asked to Curb Water Usage
quote: “Starting at 6 a.m. Monday morning and continuing through 6 p.m. Monday, March 31, residents are asked to curb non-essential outdoor and indoor water use,” (per WCAX).
commentary: The Champlain Water District has to tackle a major pipeline project, and they’d rather not run dry in the middle of it all. If you’re in South Burlington or Shelburne, think twice about any extra-long showers this week. With luck, cutting back now means fewer headaches later—no one wants to scrounge for bottled water at midnight.

headline: Young chefs prepare for weekend competition
quote: “I am actually a terrible cook. That’s the reason why I took foods this year.” (per WCAX)
commentary: Essex High School students are gearing up to face off at the 17th Annual Junior Iron Chef VT competition, and it seems half the fun is just rolling up their sleeves and trying out new dishes. It’s refreshing to see so many young folks diving into local food culture—although we’ll see which kitchen novices transform into top-tier taco artists come Saturday morning.

headline: Nomad Coffee Owner and Baker Chris Johnson Dies Unexpectedly
quote: “He was a very rich, textured person. We’re just feeling at a loss.” (per Seven Days)
commentary: Chris Johnson’s untimely passing has left a big hole in Burlington’s coffee and pastry scene. Locals who adored his artisan croissants and warm sense of humor will feel that absence each morning. If you were lucky enough to snag a treat from Nomad, you know just how special his baked goods were. I can personally tell you that those chocolate croissants and Kouign-amann’s are to die for. Rest easy Chris.

headline: Ben & Jerry’s corporate employees hold protest walkout
quote: “Over 40 employees held a silent protest in support of independence… in pursuing the company’s progressive social mission.” (per WCAX)
commentary: The famous ice cream maker’s staff took a stand against corporate constraints with a midday march. Whether you side with Unilever or the local brand’s push for autonomy, it’s clear that folks in Vermont take both democracy and dairy very seriously. We’ll see how this corporate swirl shakes out in the next few weeks.

headline: UVM Medical Center and Green Mountain Care Board reach settlement in budgeting dispute
quote: “The proposed agreement will not change the suspension of the construction of UVMMC’s new surgery center… or most other cuts announced in November.” (per WCAX)
commentary: While this settlement adds some clarity to the financial puzzle, it doesn’t exactly roll back the serious cuts already on the table. Still, local officials hope these moves stabilize a hospital system that’s been strapped for funds and facing tough decisions. Fingers crossed this is a step toward healthier budgets—and healthier care—for Vermonters.

headline: Two more University of Vermont men’s basketball players enter the NCAA transfer portal
quote: “Alamutu was one of Vermont's top facilitators in the 2024-2025 season, leading the team in assists (73).” (per MyNBC5)
commentary: The Catamounts will be shaking up their roster as another pair of athletes pursue new hardwood horizons. UVM might be losing a key distributor, but that just means younger players get a chance to step up. No doubt the benches in Patrick Gym will be buzzing with talk of next season’s potential lineup changes.

headline: Burlington Businesses Call for Relief from Construction
quote: “On Friday morning, business owners will gather at City Hall to demand answers from Burlington leaders about the Main Street makeover.” (per WCAX)
commentary: Construction delays have a knack for throwing a wrench in everyone’s plans, and it sounds like local shops have had enough. With roadway closures dragging on, folks are hoping for better timelines and less disruption to foot traffic. Here’s hoping City Hall offers a plan that keeps the commerce flowing.

headline: Construction Resumes on Main Street in Winooski
quote: “Winooski residents say it marks the end of a peaceful and lucrative winter season.” (per WCAX)
commentary: Winter offered a rare stretch of calm for Winooski businesses—but the cones and crews are back. As traffic headaches return, local spots are bracing for another round of slow days and spoiled produce. If you’re headed that way, maybe build in some extra time and show them a little love.

headline: Part of Highway Linking Vermont and Montreal Scrapped
quote: “A spokesperson tells us that money for the final 2.7 miles will go to other transportation priorities.” (per WCAX)
commentary: So much for that breezy 25-mile sprint to Montreal. While the partial highway is saving time for some, the dream of a complete direct route is off the table. It might mean a couple more pit stops when you head north for poutine, but at least the open road is still open—just not as open as we’d hoped.

headline: Students Show Off Their Skills in Robotics Competition
quote: “More than 500 students from all over New England participated in Vermont’s Robotics Competition.” (per WCAX)
